mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings (RSAFS) indicate expanding household balance sheets, which sustain consumption demand and support corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising RSAFS periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising UNRATE, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and industrial production reflects synchronized demand and supply weakness, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when both HOUST and INDPRO decline simultaneously compared to periods where only one declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, depressing valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 during tightening periods, or 60-day forward returns in tightening cycles are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher during periods of rising INDPRO, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1300 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive forward returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST drops below 1300, or the signal fails to predict returns with a t-statistic magnitude greater than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between strong employment growth and rising inflation indicates an overheating economy that forces aggressive monetary policy, ultimately compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, accelerating real GDP growth combined with elevated inflation expectations leads to valuation compression and earnings volatility, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during late-cycle GDP acceleration phases, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample walk-forward testing over 3 consecutive years.
